pertinent_positives.addl-hx: pill-rolling 4-6 Hz rest tremor worse at rest and improved with movement, micrographia, shuffling gait, reduced sense of smell, failure to respond to sertraline
pertinent_positives.pe: masked facies, reduced blink rate, cogwheel rigidity, bradykinesia, shuffling gait with reduced arm swing, positive pull test
pertinent_positives.labs: normal CBC, CMP, TSH ruling out metabolic and thyroid causes
pertinent_positives.imaging: MRI brain without acute findings, no midbrain atrophy
pertinent_positives.special: DaTscan showing reduced dopamine transporter uptake in bilateral putamen consistent with nigrostriatal degeneration

2026-04-06.plan: initiate levodopa/carbidopa 25/100mg TID, taper sertraline, refer to movement disorder specialist, occupational therapy referral, outpatient follow-up in 4-6 weeks

2026-04-07.pmh: hypertension, GERD, and 45 pack-year smoking history
2026-04-07.presenting: a 3-month progressive cough with hemoptysis and 12 lb unintentional weight loss

pertinent_positives.addl-hx: progressive dry cough, two episodes of hemoptysis, 12 lb weight loss despite normal appetite, morning facial swelling worse with bending forward, collar too tight, morning headaches, proximal muscle weakness, diplopia
pertinent_positives.pe: plethoric cyanotic facies, bilateral upper extremity and facial edema, dilated non-pulsatile neck veins, chest wall venous collaterals flowing downward, decreased right upper lobe breath sounds, proximal muscle weakness with diminished reflexes
pertinent_positives.labs: hyponatremia Na 128 with inappropriately elevated urine Na consistent with SIADH, normocytic anemia Hgb 10.4, mildly elevated LFTs
pertinent_positives.imaging: CXR and CT chest showing 6.2cm right hilar mass with 80% SVC compression and extensive mediastinal lymphadenopathy
pertinent_positives.special: bronchoscopy biopsy confirming small cell carcinoma, Ki-67 >80%, synaptophysin and chromogranin A positive, extensive stage

2026-04-07.assessment: Small Cell Lung Cancer, Extensive Stage — complicated by SVC syndrome, SIADH, and Lambert-Eaton Myasthenic Syndrome
2026-04-07.plan: admit for stabilization, head of bed elevation, IV dexamethasone, fluid restriction 800-1000 mL/day for SIADH, urgent oncology consult, expedite tissue diagnosis confirmation, initiate carboplatin/etoposide/atezolizumab upon histologic confirmation

2026-04-08.pmh: bicuspid aortic valve diagnosed at age 22, no prior interventions
2026-04-08.presenting: 3 weeks of daily fevers, night sweats, and 9 lb weight loss following a dental cleaning without antibiotic prophylaxis

pertinent_positives.addl-hx: dental procedure 5 weeks ago without prophylaxis, known bicuspid aortic valve, pleuritic chest pain, painful erythematous nodules on fingertip pads, dark lines under fingernails, yellow eyes noted by wife
pertinent_positives.pe: new harsh diastolic murmur grade III/VI, wide pulse pressure 122/48, Osler nodes on right index and middle fingertips, splinter hemorrhages under nails, Janeway lesions on bilateral thenar eminences, Roth spots on fundoscopy, splenomegaly 2cm below costal margin
pertinent_positives.labs: leukocytosis WBC 16,800 with left shift, normocytic anemia Hgb 9.8, elevated creatinine 1.6, microscopic hematuria with RBC casts on UA consistent with immune complex glomerulonephritis, new PR prolongation to 220ms on EKG
pertinent_positives.imaging: TTE showing 1.4cm vegetation on bicuspid aortic valve with severe aortic regurgitation, bilateral peripheral nodular opacities on CXR consistent with septic emboli
pertinent_positives.special: all 3 blood culture sets positive for Streptococcus viridans, fully penicillin susceptible MIC 0.06

2026-04-08.assessment: Infective Endocarditis — native bicuspid aortic valve, Streptococcus viridans, complicated by severe aortic regurgitation, immune complex glomerulonephritis, septic pulmonary emboli, and new PR prolongation concerning for perivalvular abscess
2026-04-08.plan: blood cultures x3 before antibiotics, IV ceftriaxone 2g daily x4 weeks, urgent TEE to evaluate for perivalvular abscess given PR prolongation, cardiac surgery consult, admit to cardiology with surgery on standby, serial EKGs and echo

2026-04-09.presenting: episodic attacks of severe headache, diaphoresis, and palpitations over 8 months, with a collapse this morning

pertinent_positives.addl-hx: paroxysmal episodes triggered by exertion and bending lasting 20-40 minutes with complete resolution between attacks, 8 lb weight loss, family history of adrenal tumor in mother
pertinent_positives.pe: BP 188/112, HR 108, flushed and diaphoretic, right upper quadrant abdominal tenderness, hypertensive retinopathy
pertinent_positives.labs: hyperglycemia glucose 148 without prior diabetes, mildly elevated troponin from catecholamine-induced myocardial injury, frequent PVCs on EKG
pertinent_positives.imaging: CT abdomen showing 4.8cm heterogeneous right adrenal mass with central necrosis — highly suspicious for pheochromocytoma
pertinent_positives.special: plasma free metanephrines 2.8 nmol/L and 24-hr urine metanephrines markedly elevated, confirming biochemical diagnosis

2026-04-09.assessment: Pheochromocytoma — right adrenal gland, biochemically confirmed
2026-04-09.plan: admit for alpha-blockade with phenoxybenzamine, endocrinology and surgical oncology consult, 10-14 day preoperative preparation with high-salt diet and volume expansion, genetic testing for RET/VHL/SDHB, laparoscopic adrenalectomy after adequate preparation

2026-04-10.presenting: three weeks of progressive headache, nausea, and cognitive slowing — worse at work, better on days off — with a near-syncopal episode yesterday

pertinent_positives.addl-hx: symptoms strictly worse at work and resolve on days off, two co-workers with identical symptoms, ventilation sealed for winter, near-syncope yesterday
pertinent_positives.pe: encephalopathic, cherry-red skin tone on lips and nail beds, ataxia on tandem gait, tachycardic
pertinent_positives.labs: elevated lactate 4.2, elevated troponin from CO myocardial injury, metabolic acidosis, elevated hematocrit from compensatory polycythemia, normal SpO2 falsely reassuring
pertinent_positives.imaging: CT head without acute intracranial process, subtle cerebral edema
pertinent_positives.special: COHb 28% confirming carbon monoxide poisoning — normal SpO2 was misleading

2026-04-10.assessment: Carbon Monoxide Poisoning — occupational, chronic subacute exposure with acute-on-chronic episode, complicated by myocardial injury and encephalopathy
2026-04-10.plan: 100% O2 via non-rebreather mask, admit for cardiac monitoring, hyperbaric oxygen therapy (COHb >25% with neurological symptoms), serial troponins, neurology follow-up for delayed sequelae, mandatory reporting and evacuation of furnace hall

2026-04-11.pmh: prior spontaneous left pneumothorax 18 months ago, not formally treated
2026-04-11.presenting: sudden left-sided chest pain and dyspnea at rest — second episode of spontaneous pneumothorax

pertinent_positives.addl-hx: second ipsilateral episode, first ignored 18 months ago, tall lean habitus BMI 19, no trauma or exertion precipitant
pertinent_positives.pe: decreased breath sounds and hyperresonance left hemithorax, trachea midline, O2 95%
pertinent_positives.labs: mild hypoxemia pO2 78 on ABG, low voltage on EKG left leads consistent with PTX
pertinent_positives.imaging: CXR showing 30% left pneumothorax with visible pleural line
pertinent_positives.special: CT chest confirming multiple bilateral subpleural blebs, largest 1.4cm left upper lobe — surgical bleb disease confirmed

2026-04-11.assessment: Primary Spontaneous Pneumothorax — second episode, bilateral bleb disease on CT, surgical intervention indicated
2026-04-11.plan: needle aspiration or small-bore chest tube, supplemental O2, admit for observation and repeat CXR, thoracic surgery consult for VATS pleurodesis, Marfan syndrome assessment

2026-04-12.presenting: acute anticholinergic toxidrome following two hours of unprotected dermal exposure to Atropa belladonna during herbalist work

pertinent_positives.addl-hx: two hours bare-handed grinding of belladonna berries and leaves, facial contact, symptom onset within two hours — blurred vision first, then dry mouth, then palpitations, now urinary retention
pertinent_positives.pe: fixed maximally dilated pupils 8mm bilaterally, flushed hot dry skin with complete anhidrosis, HR 128, temp 38.6, distended bladder with 680mL retention on ultrasound, absent bowel sounds
pertinent_positives.labs: mildly elevated glucose, QT prolongation to 480ms on EKG — arrhythmia risk
pertinent_positives.imaging: CT head without acute intracranial process, bladder scan confirming urinary retention
pertinent_positives.special: serum and urine toxicology positive for atropine and tropane alkaloids confirming belladonna anticholinergic toxidrome

2026-04-12.assessment: Anticholinergic Toxidrome — Atropa belladonna dermal and mucosal exposure, complicated by urinary retention, ileus, hyperthermia, and QT prolongation
2026-04-12.plan: IV benzodiazepines for agitation, urinary catheterization, active cooling, continuous telemetry for QT monitoring, IV magnesium on standby, physostigmine if refractory agitation or hemodynamic compromise, toxicology consult, admit for 24-48hr monitoring
